name: Reencuentros Terremotos Venezuela
db_description:
## **Nuestra propuesta de valor**

Plataforma que centraliza reportes visuales (fotos y videos) recientes y georreferenciados de quienes necesitan ser encontrados —personas y animales—. Esta información es capturada en el terreno por medios de comunicación acreditados e instituciones de ayuda autorizadas. Ponemos la inteligencia artificial al servicio de esta causa para procesar grandes volúmenes de datos e identificar coincidencias que faciliten reencuentros seguros y oportunos, asegurando que solo las organizaciones certificadas validen cada hallazgo dentro del sistema para una gestión privada de la información.

## **Nuestro norte**

Tener completamente ajustados los protocolos de ciberseguridad, protección de datos sensibles, bienestar animal y no revictimización. La seguridad de las personas y los animales siempre estará por encima de la velocidad de lanzamiento.

[Reporte cierre de hackathon Build 4 Venezuela | 28-junio-2026
](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1ROkUtXL9PnpGrWa12x4zw6e7_sWmyfjEA_1AJ4x44QE/edit?tab=t.0#heading=h.3o36mzss0moq)

## ¡Gracias!

Esta es la estructura que lidera la fase actual. Sin embargo, queremos expresar nuestra más sincera gratitud a los múltiples profesionales que participaron previamente. Sus valiosos aportes y dedicación han sido fundamentales para alcanzar los objetivos trazados.

analysis.summary: A genuinely substantial, actively developed platform (287 commits, deployed to Vercel+Render) that centralizes missing-persons reports for Venezuela earthquake victims: a React 19/TS frontend and an Express 5/TS/Mongoose backend with JWT+Google OAuth auth, BullMQ/Redis job queues, MinIO/S3 storage, pluggable AI providers (Anthropic/OpenAI/Gemini) for text structuring, and cron jobs ingesting real external data sources (USGS, GDACS, FIRMS, INAMEH, Cruz Roja, Proteccion Civil, Venezuela Te Busca, VenezuelaReporta) with deduplication via SHA-256 idHash and upsert-based reconciliation.
analysis.project_type: full-stack web app (missing-persons/disaster platform) with separate front/ (React+Vite) and back/ (Express+Mongoose) services

infra_deploy: GitHub Actions CI (build only, no test/lint gate)
Deployed: frontend on Vercel, backend on Render

architecture.pattern: layered service architecture (routes -> validators -> services -> models) with separate BullMQ workers for async AI processing and disaster-data sync jobs; monorepo with front/ and back/ as independent deployable services
architecture.orm_or_db_layer: Mongoose ODM against MongoDB, with defined schemas, compound/geospatial indexes, and Zod-based request validation layered on top of Mixed-type fields
architecture.api_design: REST API under /api/* (persons, disasters, media, auth, admin, partners, localizados, contacts, cne, webhooks) with role- and API-key-based route protection
architecture.separation_of_concerns: Clear separation between routes, validators (Zod), services, models, adapters (external data source normalization), jobs (cron ingestion), queues/workers (BullMQ async processing), middlewares (auth, CSRF, audit)
architecture.modularity: Good modularity: dedicated adapter pattern for external sources, an AI provider abstraction (ai.interface/ai.factory) supporting 3 vendors, and per-source job files for each disaster/person data feed
notable_patterns: idempotent fetch->transform->idHash->upsert pattern for deduplicating person records across sources
double-submit CSRF token pattern with httpOnly JWT cookie
audit logging middleware for security-sensitive actions
separate BullMQ worker process/container to isolate CPU-heavy AI work from the API

code_organization.directory_structure: Clean, conventional layout separating front/back, with backend further split into routes/services/models/validators/adapters/jobs/queues/workers/middlewares/utils — well organized for a project of this size (~13k LOC).
code_organization.naming_quality: Consistent, descriptive kebab-case/PascalCase naming across files (e.g. unified-person.model.ts, ia-processor.worker.ts, reconciliation.service.ts) that reflects real domain concepts.
code_organization.documentation_quality: Extensive Spanish-language docs: Readme.md, DEVELOPERS.md, TDD.md, Integraciones.md, plus a Doc/index.html architecture doc — unusually thorough for a hackathon entry, with inline code comments explaining security tradeoffs (e.g. CSRF cookie rationale).
code_organization.reasoning: Directory layout, naming, and documentation all reflect deliberate engineering practice rather than generated scaffolding; minor deduction because some scripts (scraper-venezuelareporta.js) mix JS into an otherwise all-TS codebase.

production_readiness.secrets_handling: Uses .env files (gitignored) with a documented .env.example listing only variable names, fail-fast startup checks for JWT_SECRET/WEBHOOK_API_KEY, and explicit warnings never to commit secrets/PII to the public repo.
production_readiness.security_notes: Strong for a hackathon project: Helmet with custom CSP, HSTS, CORS allowlist, hpp, express-rate-limit (global + strict auth-specific), CSRF double-submit protection, JWT with tokenVersion revocation, password hashing util, role-based admin middleware with JWT+legacy API-key fallback, and an audit-log middleware recording security events.
production_readiness.scalability_notes: Multi-stage Docker build, separate worker container for AI processing decoupled from the API via BullMQ/Redis, MongoDB geospatial indexes for map queries, and docker-compose healthchecks/dependency ordering; horizontal worker scaling is explicitly documented.
production_readiness.reasoning: Recent commits (same day as review) show active production deployment fixes (Vercel proxy rewrites, trust proxy for secure cookies behind Render's load balancer, sameSite=none for cross-origin cookies), indicating the app is live and being hardened in real time, though CI only builds (no automated test/lint gate) and the live homepage currently returned HTTP 404 at check time.

maturity.reasoning: 287 commits from 4 contributors, 20 backend test files (unit tests with mocked Mongoose models covering services/routes/middlewares/queues/workers), frontend Vitest+RTL tests, real third-party integrations (USGS, GDACS, FIRMS, Google OAuth, Anthropic/OpenAI/Gemini, Pinecone), and CI pipeline building both front and back — this is far beyond boilerplate, though CI does not run the test suites.

viability.reasoning: Directly and concretely addresses the hackathon's core need (family reunification and disaster alerting for Venezuela) with working deduplication logic, geospatial search, real disaster-data feeds, and a live deployment; main risks to production use are unverified data-source reliability/legal access (scraping third-party sites), the live URL returning 404 at check time, and reliance on paid third-party AI/vector APIs for core matching functionality.

key_features: Centralized, deduplicated missing-persons registry (UnifiedPerson) with SHA-256 idHash upsert logic
Multi-source ingestion jobs for earthquakes/disasters (USGS, GDACS, FIRMS, INAMEH) and missing persons (Venezuela Te Busca, Cruz Roja, Proteccion Civil, VenezuelaReporta, web-form/WhatsApp adapters)
AI-assisted structuring of free-text reports via a pluggable Anthropic/OpenAI/Gemini provider factory, processed asynchronously via BullMQ workers
Geospatial map search using MongoDB 2dsphere indexes and Leaflet clustering on the frontend
Google OAuth + email/password auth with JWT (httpOnly cookie), CSRF protection, role-based access, and admin/partner/webhook API-key layers
Human-in-the-loop match/reconciliation service (matcher.service, reconciliation.service) rather than auto-notification
Admin dashboard, verified-organization directory, and a documented humanitarian/seismic-safety manual in the frontend
analysis.merge_potential: Strong standalone candidate rather than a merge target — it already implements a full missing-persons pipeline (ingestion, dedup, AI structuring, geospatial search, auth) that other people-finder or seismic-data hackathon entries could plausibly consume via its adapter/webhook pattern instead of duplicating effort.
red_flags: Live homepage (reencuentro-terremoto-venezuela.vercel.app) returned HTTP 404 at time of review, suggesting deployment/config issues despite same-day fix commits
CI pipeline only builds front/back — it does not run the Jest/Vitest test suites, so regressions in the 20+ backend test files are not enforced
Relies on scraping third-party government/NGO sites (scripts/scraper-venezuelareporta.js) whose legality/stability is unverified
requireAdminApiKey retains a legacy static API-key admin bypass alongside JWT auth, widening the attack surface if that key leaks
Core deduplication/matching depends on paid external AI and vector-DB APIs (Anthropic/OpenAI/Gemini, Pinecone), creating cost and availability risk for a humanitarian-critical feature

diffusion.recommended_angle: Position as a serious, security-hardened humanitarian-tech infrastructure story (287 commits, real USGS/GDACS/Cruz Roja data ingestion, AI-assisted matching) rather than a consumer-facing demo — appeal to technical/donor audiences and potential partner NGOs once the live site is fixed, since there is nothing visually demoable to the public right now.
gaps: The public-facing frontend (reencuentro-terremoto-venezuela.vercel.app) returns HTTP 404 right now, so there is no live product a victim or donor can actually open today
The 'live_url' field in the project record actually points to a private/editable Google Doc (a TDD/testing document titled 'TDD_Reencuentro VE'), not a live demo of the product
No screenshots or visual assets captured/available for social content
No evidence of real users or field usage yet (still in active development, created only days ago)

evaluation.live_demo_notes: The 'live_url' supplied for this project is not the actual product but a private Google Doc ('TDD_Reencuentro VE', a testing/spec document) with unsaved-changes state — it is not a demo of the platform. The actual deployed frontend referenced in the technical analysis (reencuentro-terremoto-venezuela.vercel.app) returns HTTP 404 Not Found when fetched now, confirming the red flag already noted in the insight file (deployment/config issues despite same-day fix commits). No working, publicly reachable version of the product could be verified.
evaluation.one_line_pitch: Reencuentros VE: una plataforma con IA que cruza reportes de organizaciones certificadas, Cruz Roja y datos sísmicos en tiempo real (USGS, GDACS) para reencontrar de forma segura a personas y animales desaparecidos tras el terremoto en Venezuela.
evaluation.real_problem_reasoning: Family/animal reunification after a mass-casualty earthquake is one of the most urgent, life-and-safety-critical needs for disaster victims (locating missing loved ones, avoiding scams/misinformation, coordinating with verified relief organizations). The project's architecture — deduplicated missing-persons registry, multi-source ingestion from real disaster/humanitarian feeds (USGS, GDACS, FIRMS, INAMEH, Cruz Roja, Protección Civil, Venezuela Te Busca), AI-assisted structuring of free-text field reports, geospatial search, and a human-in-the-loop verification/reconciliation step (rather than blind auto-matching) — shows the team engineered directly against this real problem and its risks (misidentification, revictimization, data privacy), not a generic hackathon idea.
